title: euonymus aculeatus
intro: Euonymus aculeatus, also known as prickly spindle tree, is a deciduous shrub native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its thorny branches and red or yellowish bark. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Euonymus aculeatus include its ability to reduce fever, soothe skin irritations, and support the immune system. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves and bark in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
